name: Semiconductors (SMH/SOX)
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **SMH $549.76 (+5.2% 1D, +37.5% 1M, +35.2% 3M) — AI-CYCLE COMPOUNDING + APPLE-SAMSUNG-INTEL CHIP-PRODUCTION TALKS HALO INTACT.** Bloomberg May 5 confirmed Apple has held early talks with Intel and Samsung about producing iPhone main-device chips in the US, currently relying almost entirely on TSMC Taiwan. Critical caveat per Bloomberg: 'talks remain preliminary and no orders have been placed' — rally is anticipatory. Samsung Q1 2026 net profits 57.2T KRW (~$39B) — single-quarter higher than ALL of 2025 combined. Samsung market cap crossed $1T May 6. KOSPI 7,485.20 (+1.4% 1D, +37.3% 1M, +41.3% 3M); TAIEX 41,933.78 (+1.9% 1D, +26.2% 1M, +29.4% 3M). **NIKKEI +5.7% MAY 7 IS THE INTERNATIONAL VALIDATION OF AI-CYCLE DECOUPLING:** Nikkei reopened from Golden Week absorbing 3 sessions of US AI records in single day (SoftBank +13-16%, Advantest +8%, Tokyo Electron +9%); USD/JPY ¥156.22 (slightly stronger yen, NOT yen-weakness move); BOJ explicitly NOT the driver. Crisis-linked %: ~30% peace dividend; ~70% AI-cycle decoupling (consistent with Patel/SemiAnalysis thesis). Japanese tech now trading on same factor model as NASDAQ. **For India deployment thesis:** AI flows continue to bypass India IT services and rotate to Korea/Taiwan/US tech — Indian-equity allocation continues to underperform structurally in this regime. Patel/SemiAnalysis bifurcation thesis sharpens; Apple-Samsung-Intel talks specifically signal a US-AI-manufacturing-onshore tailwind that will rotate flows further from Indian IT services.
